name: Next.js + Supabase + Upstash rate-limit
description: Test conventions for a Next.js application backed by Supabase auth and Upstash Redis rate-limiting. Extracted from production use at FirstLeap (2026-04-26 hardening session). Opt in with: branchnux codify <slug> --test-conventions nextjs-supabase

patterns.title: XFF rate-limit isolation
patterns.description: Every test.describe() block MUST have a test.beforeEach() that calls xffForTest(testInfo.title) and sets the X-Forwarded-For HTTP header on the browser context. This prevents sequential auth tests from sharing a rate-limit bucket (Upstash Redis keyed on client IP) and causing 429 poisoning across tests. Rate-limit / destructive tests MUST be placed last in the suite and annotated with // @rate-limit-test.

patterns.title: form.requestSubmit() not button.click()
patterns.description: NEVER use button.click() to submit forms. ALWAYS use form.requestSubmit() via page.evaluate(). Reason: button.click() fires before React hydration attaches the onSubmit handler in production builds (next build && next start), causing a native browser GET instead of the POST the app expects. This is a Next.js hydration race, not a Playwright bug.

patterns.title: afterEach evidence capture via captureEvidence()
patterns.description: Include the top-level afterEach hook that matches the TC-ID from testInfo.title and calls captureEvidence(). Tests with custom browser contexts MUST call captureEvidence() before ctx.close() — if the context closes first, the screenshot cannot be taken. The captureEvidence() helper writes a screenshot to evidence/<TC-ID>.png and appends a JSONL record to evidence/execution-log-auto.md.

patterns.title: waitForNextTotpWindow — TOTP collision guard
patterns.description: Include the waitForNextTotpWindow() helper if ANY test in the plan involves TOTP/MFA. Supabase's TOTP endpoint returns 429 if the same OTP window is used twice within a 30-second period. waitForNextTotpWindow() pauses until the next 30-second TOTP window boundary before generating a fresh OTP, preventing 429 from hitting the challenge endpoint twice in the same window.
